Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, with elevated creatinine suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glycosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible renal tubular dysfunction (e.g., Fanconi syndrome) or early diabetic nephropathy, though diabetes is unlikely given low glucose/HbA1c. No evidence of infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or renal tubular disorders.
